links.summary: NVIDIA and Meta announced a multi-year partnership in February 2026 for the large-scale deployment of Blackwell and Rubin GPUs. This deal includes the 'Grace' CPU-only deployment to support Meta's advanced ad-targeting AI agents.

NVIDIA officially overtook Apple as TSMC's largest customer in early 2026 due to surging AI chip demand. While NVIDIA supplies the hardware for Apple's internal AI training, they compete for limited advanced semiconductor manufacturing nodes.

Google’s new 'Ironwood' TPU v7 chips are positioned as the primary merchant silicon alternative to NVIDIA’s GPUs. Despite this, Google Cloud remains a major provider of NVIDIA H100/B200 clusters for third-party developers.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) remains a top-tier customer for NVIDIA's Blackwell architecture to power its Bedrock AI platform. Amazon also utilizes NVIDIA networking hardware to connect its massive data center clusters.

Apple is reportedly expanding its ties with Google to integrate Gemini AI models directly into Siri for the 2026 software cycle. Simultaneously, they compete in mobile OS (iOS vs. Android) and the emerging AI-search market.

The two companies are locked in a 'clash of visions' regarding the future of spatial computing and AR glasses. Apple’s Vision Pro and upcoming AR wearables compete directly with Meta’s Quest 3 and Orion prototypes.

Apple and Amazon compete for smart home dominance via HomeKit and Alexa, though they cooperate on the 'Matter' interoperability standard. Reports in 2026 suggest Apple is leaning on AWS cloud infrastructure for some Siri backend storage.

Meta and Google are the world's two largest AI-driven advertising platforms, fighting for the same marketing budgets. They also lead competing open-source AI ecosystems, with Meta's Llama models rivaling Google's Gemma.

These two are the primary rivals in the Cloud AI 'Wars,' with AWS and Google Cloud racing to sign exclusive enterprise deals. They also compete in consumer hardware, specifically in the AI-enhanced smart speaker market.

Meta relies on AWS for secondary cloud capacity and as a distribution channel for its Llama models via Amazon Bedrock. They collaborate on optimizing AI model performance for AWS-specific 'Trainium' and 'Inferentia' chips.
